content: The linguistic pedagogy definition of reflexion as a deliberate, vocabulary-rooted metacognitive practice for learning can be borrowed to improve current LLM self-reflection mechanisms, which currently lack structured guided reflection grounded in explicit linguistic framing. Just as high school students build better critical writing skills through explicitly named reflection exercises that anchor metacognition to concrete vocabulary, LLMs can be fine-tuned to ground their self-correction reflection in explicit labeled linguistic frameworks for error types.
